name: Chicken with Pineapple (Bo-lo-shao-ji)
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Sake or Chinese rice wine
2/3 cup Pineapple juice from the can
4 tablespoons Cornstarch dissolved in 2 Tablespoons water
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the cavity of the chicken with a cloth.
2. Prick all over the skin with a fork. Wipe off any moisture. Place the chicken in a bowl. Pour Mixture A over the chicken and let stand for about 30 minutes.
3. Remove the chicken and reserve the marinade.
4. Heat 3 cups of frying oil to 350°F (about 175°C) in a wok. Add the chicken and fry until golden, spooning hot oil over it from time to remove any moisture. Place the chicken on a rack.
5. Pour off oil from the wok.
6. Heat 4 tablespoons fresh salad oil in the wok and saute the onions until they become fragrant. Add the reserved marinade and pineapple juice; bring to a boil. Add the water and chicken. Cover and cook for 25 minutes.
7. Remove the chicken when the liquid is reduced to about 1 cup. Add the pineapple cubes to the liquid and cook over very low heat for 15 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, cut the chicken into 2-inch pieces, leaving the bone attached. Arrange them on a platter.
9. If necessary, thicken the pineapple sauce with a little dissolved cornstarch. Sprinkle with sesame oil and stir well. Pour the sauce over the chicken, garnish with Chinese parsley (coriander) leaves, and serve.
